_meta.schema: exact: token -> {year, family, order, type, summary, good_for, pairs_with, notes, supersedes_by}; prefix: ordered list of {match (regex), family, summary, good_for, pairs_with}. Exact wins over prefix. Unknown year is null.
_meta.sources: Core sampler dates from ComfyUI git history + cited papers. RES4LYF families described at the family level — too many tokens to enumerate 1:1. Contributions welcome via PR.

euler.summary: Simplest first-order ODE solver. The baseline — fast, predictable, slightly soft.
euler.good_for: Quick previews, anime, conservative low-step counts (10-20)

euler_cfg_pp.summary: Euler with CFG++ rescaling (Chung et al). Reduces saturation and burn at high CFG.
euler_cfg_pp.good_for: When CFG > 7 is causing oversaturation; use lower CFG (2-4) with this variant

euler_ancestral.summary: Adds noise each step. Never fully converges — every step count looks different. Good for variety.

euler_ancestral_cfg_pp.summary: Euler ancestral with CFG++ rescaling. Anime/creative variety without high-CFG burn.

heun.summary: Second-order predictor-corrector. ~2× the compute per step of euler but converges with fewer steps.
heun.good_for: Quality runs at 20-30 steps when correctness matters

heunpp2.summary: Improved Heun. Slightly higher accuracy than heun at similar per-step cost.

exp_heun_2_x0.summary: Exponential-integrator Heun with x0 prediction. Recent addition; less battle-tested than dpmpp_2m.
exp_heun_2_x0.good_for: Experimental — try when default solvers stall

dpm_2.summary: Original DPM-Solver second-order. Largely superseded by dpmpp_2m for the same cost.

dpm_2_ancestral.summary: DPM-Solver-2 with noise injection. Largely superseded by dpmpp_2m_sde.

lms.summary: k-diffusion's classical linear multistep. Pre-DPM++ era; less competitive today.

dpm_fast.summary: Lu's fast adaptive DPM — chooses order per step.

dpm_adaptive.summary: Fully adaptive — ignores the step-count widget and chooses steps by error tolerance. Slow but accurate.
dpm_adaptive.good_for: Reference-quality renders; the `steps` widget is essentially ignored
dpm_adaptive.notes: step count is not honored — the solver decides

dpmpp_2s_ancestral.summary: DPM-Solver++ order-2 single-step ancestral. Good variety, decent quality.

dpmpp_sde.summary: DPM++ in SDE form — adds noise each step. Higher quality variant than dpmpp_2s_ancestral, slower per step.

dpmpp_sde_gpu.summary: dpmpp_sde with GPU-deterministic RNG. Same results on the same GPU; reproducible across runs on identical hardware.
dpmpp_sde_gpu.good_for: When reproducibility on the same GPU matters

dpmpp_2m.summary: The default workhorse. Order-2 multistep — reuses prior model evaluations, so very efficient. Converges well at 15-25 steps for most checkpoints.

dpmpp_2m_sde.summary: dpmpp_2m with stochastic noise injection. Often best-in-class quality at 20-30 steps for SDXL.

dpmpp_2m_sde_heun.summary: dpmpp_2m_sde with a Heun corrector step. Slightly higher accuracy, more compute.
dpmpp_2m_sde_heun.good_for: Quality runs when extra accuracy is worth the cost

dpmpp_2m_sde_heun_gpu.good_for: Same as dpmpp_2m_sde_heun, reproducible per GPU

dpmpp_3m_sde.summary: Order-3 multistep SDE. Higher-order than dpmpp_2m_sde; usually only worth it at 25+ steps.

dpmpp_3m_sde_gpu.good_for: Same as dpmpp_3m_sde, reproducible per GPU

ddpm.summary: Original DDPM sampler (Ho et al). Needs hundreds of steps to converge — historical reference.
ddpm.good_for: Almost never in practice; use dpmpp_2m or dpmpp_2m_sde instead

lcm.summary: Designed for LCM-LoRA-distilled checkpoints. Produces results in 4-8 steps. Useless without LCM-LoRA.

ipndm.summary: Improved Pseudo-Numerical Diffusion Methods. Valid alternative to dpmpp_2m; less popular.
ipndm.good_for: Niche use; try when dpmpp_2m fails

ipndm_v.summary: Variance-preserving iPNDM. Same niche as ipndm.

deis.summary: Diffusion Exponential Integrator Sampler (Zhang & Chen). Competitive with dpmpp_2m at low step counts.

res_multistep.summary: RES family (Hochbruck-Ostermann exponential RK), multistep. Recent core addition.
res_multistep.good_for: Alternative to dpmpp_2m worth trying for SDXL/Flux

res_multistep_ancestral.summary: RES multistep with ancestral noise injection.
res_multistep_ancestral.good_for: RES alternative to euler_a for variety

gradient_estimation.summary: Adds a gradient-estimation refinement step per sample step.
gradient_estimation.good_for: Experimental — try as an alternative to euler

er_sde.summary: Exponential-Reverse SDE solver. Recent research entry.

sa_solver.summary: Stochastic Adams solver (Xue et al). Strong few-step performance.

sa_solver_pece.summary: SA-Solver with PECE (Predict-Evaluate-Correct-Evaluate) iteration. Higher accuracy per step.
sa_solver_pece.good_for: Few-step regimes when extra accuracy is worth the cost

ddim.summary: Denoising Diffusion Implicit Models (Song et al). Deterministic, historically important. Largely superseded by DPM++ but still useful for SDXL refiners and some ControlNet workflows.
ddim.good_for: SDXL refiner, some ControlNet pipelines, img2img with strict structure preservation

uni_pc.summary: Unified Predictor-Corrector (Zhao et al). Excellent at low step counts.

uni_pc_bh2.summary: UniPC with B^h_2 coefficient variant. Slightly different convergence behavior than uni_pc.
uni_pc_bh2.good_for: Low-step regimes; try when uni_pc results look off

unipc_bh1.summary: FramePackWrapper's UniPC with B^h_1 coefficient. Same algorithm family as core uni_pc but distinct token (no underscore between 'uni' and 'pc'). FramePack's default.
unipc_bh1.good_for: FramePack image-to-video; the recommended starting point for that wrapper
unipc_bh1.notes: Same UniPC math as core uni_pc; the rename comes from FramePack's wrapper

unipc_bh2.summary: FramePackWrapper's UniPC with B^h_2 coefficient. Distinct token from core uni_pc_bh2 — same algorithm, different wrapper naming.
unipc_bh2.good_for: FramePack image-to-video; try if unipc_bh1 results feel off
unipc_bh2.notes: Same UniPC math as core uni_pc_bh2; the rename comes from FramePack's wrapper

euler_perlin.summary: Iterative-Mixer's Euler variant with Perlin-noise-modulated noise injection. Produces softer textures than plain euler in mixing workflows.
euler_perlin.good_for: ComfyUI-Iterative-Mixer workflows; some users find it pleasant for painterly or organic textures
euler_perlin.notes: Iterative-Mixer-specific; behavior outside that node is undefined

pndm.summary: Pseudo-Numerical Methods for Diffusion (Liu et al). Diffusers-naming. Predecessor to iPNDM; ComfyUI core uses the improved variant. Surfaces in FluxTrainer's sampler selector.
pndm.good_for: FluxTrainer compatibility; modern workflows usually pick ipndm or dpmpp_2m instead

euler_a.summary: Diffusers-naming alias for euler_ancestral. Same algorithm; appears in FluxTrainer and other Diffusers-derived wrappers.
euler_a.good_for: Same use cases as euler_ancestral — anime, illustrative styles, creative variety
euler_a.notes: Equivalent to core euler_ancestral; preferred name varies by wrapper

dpm_2_a.summary: Diffusers-naming alias for dpm_2_ancestral. Surfaces in FluxTrainer's sampler selector.
dpm_2_a.good_for: FluxTrainer compatibility; modern workflows often pick dpmpp_2m_sde for similar variety

dpmsolver.summary: Diffusers-naming for the original DPM-Solver. Surfaces in FluxTrainer's sampler selector.
dpmsolver.good_for: FluxTrainer compatibility; modern workflows often reach for dpmpp_2m for the same role
dpmsolver.notes: Roughly equivalent to core dpm_2 / dpmpp_2m depending on Diffusers config

dpmsingle.summary: Diffusers-naming for DPM-Solver single-step. Single-step variant evaluates the model twice per step rather than reusing past evaluations.
dpmsingle.good_for: FluxTrainer compatibility; trades efficiency for slightly different convergence character

prefix.summary: RES family (Hochbruck-Ostermann exponential RK), multistep variant. The trailing N in res_Nm is the order — higher N reuses more past evaluations.
prefix.good_for: Advanced multistep ODE solving; alternative to dpmpp_Nm

prefix.summary: RES family single-step exponential Runge-Kutta. The N in res_Ns is the stage count — more stages = more model evaluations per step = higher accuracy per step.
prefix.good_for: High-accuracy per step at low step counts; very expensive at high stage counts (8s, 10s, 15s, 16s)

prefix.summary: RES4LYF's exposed single-step DPM++ variants. Higher-order than core's dpmpp_2s_ancestral.
Cox-Matthews ETD-RK family. Treats stiff linear part exactly via matrix exponential.
prefix.good_for: Stiff regions of the diffusion trajectory; experimental

prefix.summary: Lawson exponential Runge-Kutta — generalized exponential integrators.
Adams-Bashforth predictor with Nørsett correction. Hybrid multistep family.
DEIS family with higher-order multistep variants exposed by RES4LYF.
Hybrid PEC iteration — predict, then iteratively correct.
Classical low-order Runge-Kutta variants. Educational / baseline.
prefix.good_for: Baseline comparison; rarely the best practical choice

prefix.summary: Classical Runge-Kutta family (rk4, rk5, Dormand-Prince, Bogacki-Shampine, etc). Mathematically pristine but not specialized for diffusion.
prefix.good_for: Reference / curiosity; expensive at high stage counts

prefix.summary: Implicit Runge-Kutta — Gauss-Legendre points. Highest order per stage, but each step requires an iterative solve.
Implicit Runge-Kutta — Radau points. L-stable, good for stiff problems.
Implicit Runge-Kutta — Lobatto points. Includes both endpoints in the stencil.
Diagonally-implicit and IMEX Runge-Kutta variants. Cheaper than fully-implicit Gauss-Legendre/Radau/Lobatto.
prefix.good_for: Compromise between accuracy and per-step cost; experimental
